sources.title: Superman (1940s animated film series) Directed by Dave Fleischer
sources.description:
The Fleischer Superman cartoons are a series of 17 Technicolor animated shorts released by Paramount Pictures, marking Superman’s first appearance in animation. Produced initially by Fleischer Studios (1941–1942) and later by Famous Studios (1942–1943), they were the last animated series created by Fleischer before the studio’s transition. Now in the U.S. public domain, the original 35mm masters and merchandising rights are owned by Warner Bros.

sources.title: SuperMan (1940s cartoon series) [Colorized, 4K, 60FPS]
sources.description: Superman cartoon series is a 1941-1943 black and white movie film which has been colorised, upscaled to 4K, and frames per second boosted to 60 FPS in 2021 using Artificial Intelligence (A.I) and Google Colab @  https://colab.research.google.com   Technologies used: A.I. Colorizing:  https://github.com/jantic/DeOldify  A.I. Increasing frame rate 60 FPS:  https://rife-vfi.github.io   A.I. Upscaling to 4K:  https://ffmpeg.org   GPU: Google Colab. This movie is in public domain category and it is Copyright free.

metadata.Plot: Superman must reconcile his alien Kryptonian heritage with his human upbringing as reporter Clark Kent. As the embodiment of truth, justice and the human way he soon finds himself in a world that views these as old-fashioned.
metadata.Writer: James Gunn, Jerry Siegel, Joe Shuster
metadata.Actors: David Corenswet, Rachel Brosnahan, Nicholas Hoult
